text: A conceptual conversation, not a coding session: the user asked Claude to characterize what kind of system the openreview/living-workspace repo actually is â€” emphasizing that it is not a conventional installable application but a living, agent-operated workspace â€” and then to situate that idea within current AI architectures and trends. Claude produced framing describing the repo as a durable, human-and-agent-shared workspace (data, schemas, tools, and views that an agent operates rather than a packaged app), and was explicitly cautioned not to overreach and to ask when unsure about how it relates to prevailing AI patterns. No files were edited or created; the output was explanatory positioning language about the living-workspace concept and its fit with agentic/AI-workspace trends.
